title: impatiens digitata subsp. jaegeri
intro: Impatiens digitata subsp. jaegeri, also known as Jaeger's jewelweed, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the balsam family. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it effective in treating skin irritations, wounds, and infections. The health benefits of Impatiens digitata subsp. jaegeri include its ability to reduce swelling and promote healing, as well as its potential to alleviate symptoms of eczema and other skin conditions. Herbal preparations of Jaeger's jewelweed can be found in topical creams, ointments, and salves, which can be applied directly to the affected area to harness its medicinal properties.
